titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L1552
GA L1552
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Available for noncommercial re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators and the subsequent derivative is licensed as ShareAlike (Creative Commons by-nc-sa)

projectDesc.p: This transcription was initially made by the Byzantine Text of John Project in 2004-7, led by Roderic L. Mullen, and later adopted for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John.

The IGNTP transcription file was duplicated for the standalone version of the Byzantine Text of John on 9.7.2014. This means that there may be slight discrepancies between the 2007 file used to prepared the Byzantine apparatus and the 2014 transcription in the present file.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Word break change at 19.37 by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L 1552 (St Ρetersburg -Russ Νat. Lib. (-ex Spiritual Αcad., b 1-5))Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1r - lectionary available through Ρalm Sunday reading only, in photocopy. Missing pages transcribed by acm. Τranscribed by: RLΜΤranscription begun: 12.07.02Τranscription finished: 19.10.02Τransference to Collate by: RLΜΤransference to Collate begun: 12.07.02Τransference to Collate finished: 19.10.02File date and time: Ρlaced in Level1: RLΜLevel1 file date and time: 19.10.02Ρlaced in Level2: read a second time and compared with the collation of Johannes Κaravidopoulos. Ηoly Week and Μenologion ΝΟΤ read in Μuenster - no film thereΡlaced in Level2 by: RLΜLevel2 file date and time: 04.12.02Τhese files were compared and differences resolved by:Ρlaced in Level3:Ρlaced in Level3 by:Final transcription file date and time: Page layout added by: acmStart date: 1.11.13End date: 5.11.13Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - Yes - but not in the Johannine material. Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No. Other remarks:

there appears to be a change in appearance of the hand towards the end of this manuscript which is possibly caused by trying to fit more text onto each page. It gradually changes throughout the 280s and 290s, until the final lection on F300 looks quite different.

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,35-39 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D6)
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: A note here marks the start of the reading for M5 D27a.
A note here marks the start of the reading for M1 D13a.
A note here marks the start of the reading for M6 D23.
A note here marks the start of the reading for M1 D14.

div.note: John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
A note here marks the start of the reading for R11 and M1 D26.
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτoυργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day
John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)
The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
This pericope is read on the Sunday that falls between 13th and 19th September in the Byzantine Church's calendar. It commemorates the Church councils.

ab.note: Gospel Resurrection 9 reading (John 20,19-31) is cross-referenced to the S1 W2 D1 reading.
Gospel Resurrection 11 reading (John 21,14-21) is cross-referenced to the S1 W7 D7 reading.
